CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT VLADIGOR INVESTMENTS, INC. DBA TOWER CAR WASH JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 7.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT VLADIGOR INVESTMENTS, INC. DBA TOWER CAR WASH DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO 1ST, 2ND, 3RD, AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE THAT HE WAS OTHERWISE QUALIFIED FOR THE JOB UNDER SCOTCH VS. ART INSTITUTE OF CA (2009) 173 CAL. APP. 4TH 986. AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION (IIED) PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD DISCRIMINATORY CONDUCT THAT VIOLATES FEHA. AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION (FAILURE TO PAY WAGES) PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD IN COMPLIANCE WITH OPPENHEIMER VS. MOEBIUS (1957) 151 CAL.APP. 2ND 818. AS TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION (B&P SEC. 17200) PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD LABOR CODE VIOLATION BASIS. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

DEFENDANT TO LODGE COURTESY COPY OF DEMURRER. (RE NOTICE OF SETTLEMENT PLAINTIFF FILED ON 11/09/10 THAT WAS REFERENCED IN THE TENTATIVE RULING, MR. SHPELFOGEL INDICATED THAT PARTIES BELIEVED THAT THEY HAD REACHED A SETTLEMENT. HOWEVER WHEN PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL MET WITH THE CLIENT TO GET HIS FINAL APPROVAL AND SIGNATURE, THE CLIENT CHANGED HIS MIND.) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT VLADIGOR INVESTMENTS, INC. DBA TOWER CAR WASH'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT A SHAM PLEADING BECAUSE THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING ABILITY TO WORK ARE NOT IRRECONCILABLE WITH THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE PRIOR COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF HAS ADEQUATELY ALLEGED THAT HE COULD PERFORM THE JOB FROM WHICH HE WAS TERMINATED. NONE OF THE CLAIMS ARE BARRED BY THE WORKERS COMPENSATION ACT. ALL OF THE CLAIMS ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR # 11918

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-18-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ASHWIN LADVA, TEL. NO. 296-8844 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RAFAEL SHPELFOGEL, TEL. NO. (323) 651-0560 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT VLADIGOR INVESTMENTS, INC. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT VLADIGOR INVESTMENTS, INC. DBA TOWER CAR WASH'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Overruled in its entirety. The second amended complaint is not a sham pleading because the allegations regarding ability to work are not irreconcilable with the allegations in the prior complaint. Plaintiff has adequately alleged that he could perform the job from which he was terminated. None of the claims are barred by the Workers Compensation Act. All of the claims are adequately pled. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR # 11918 =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT VLADIGOR INVESTMENTS, INC. dba TOWER CAR WASH DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Rafael Shpelfogel, Esq. Attorney at Law, (323) 651-0560 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendant and Moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to 1st, 2nd, 3rd, and 4th causes of action. Plaintiff to allege that he was otherwise qualified for the job under Scotch vs. Art Institute of CA (2009) 173 Cal. App. 4th 986. As to the 5th cause of action (IIED) plaintiff must plead discriminatory conduct that violates FEHA. As to the 6th cause of action (failure to pay wages) plaintiff must plead in compliance with Oppenheimer vs. Moebius (1957) 151 Cal.App. 2nd 818. As to the 7th cause of action (B&P sec. 17200) plaintiff must plead labor code violation basis. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
